data.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this exemplar child; whole sibling roster considered). This child is the rule-enabler/allowlist-author of the epic: it turns on BLE001 + scoped T201 in pyproject.toml and authors the per-file-ignores allowlist "covering all un-swept packages," and carries a "CI is green" AC at enable-time. That allowlist is implicit shared state for the whole roster (each sweep removes its package; close-out 0ec1 empties it), so this pairing is where the allowlist's initial completeness is contracted. Two high-confidence cross-child findings; other modes clean (see end).

---
FINDING 1 — Dependency gap / scope coverage: this child's "CI is green" AC and the allowlist it authors have no owner for src/rebar/_io/, a package that carries gated BLE001 sites and is in NO sweep's file_impact.

criteria: [this child AC3 "pyproject.toml enables BLE001 + T201 with a per-file-ignores allowlist covering all un-swept packages; CI is green"; this child What "an allowlist that exempts every not-yet-swept package"; parent AC5 "The allowlist is emptied; make lint + the full test suite pass"; parent SC3 "future joe-janitor audit finds no unguarded blind-except cluster"]
location: src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py:225,237,243,258,271,276,289 (7 `except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001` sites) vs. the file_impact of every sweep sibling.
evidence:
  - Sweep partition by file_impact (read from each ticket): 8d01 {__init__.py, reducer/, graph/, _store/}; d360 {grounding/, _engine_support/}; ceaa {mcp_server.py, llm/workflow/, llm/ top-level}; fd72 {_cli/, _commands/}; 0569 {_engine/rebar_reconciler/}; this child {llm/plan_review/}.
  - `src/rebar/_io/` is in NONE of those file_impacts (directory listing confirms a distinct `_io/` package; grep confirms the 7 pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001` sites above, several over a best-effort body).
  - Cross-checked other top-level un-swept files for absence: `src/rebar/_engine.py` and `src/rebar/config.py` have 0 `except Exception` (grep) — so they are NOT a hole. `_io/` is the one verified-unowned package with gated sites.
scenarios:
  - For THIS child's AC3 "CI is green" to hold the moment BLE001 turns on, this child's authored allowlist MUST enumerate `_io/` (un-swept, holds BLE001 sites). The "exempts every not-yet-swept package" rule covers it only if the author treats `_io/` as a package needing exemption — a live correctness obligation on this pairing: omit `_io/` and this child's own CI-green AC fails at enable-time.
  - Downstream: because no sweep owns `_io/`, it never leaves the allowlist, so close-out 0ec1's "empty the allowlist" is unreachable from within the planned roster. This pairing seeds the gap (authors the allowlist whose only exit path for `_io/` is undefined).
impact: A package coverage hole rooted at the allowlist this child authors: `_io/` (>=7 BLE001 sites) must be in the initial allowlist for this child's CI-green AC, yet has no owning sweep to bring it to the floor and remove it. Absence cross-checked against the whole roster — confirmed no sibling lists `_io/` / `import_ndjson.py` in file_impact.
checklist_item: dependency gap; scope coverage
suggested_fix: (a) Explicitly enumerate `src/rebar/_io/` in the initial allowlist this child authors (so its CI-green AC is satisfiable), and (b) assign `_io/` to an owning sweep (e.g. 8d01 / d360 or a new "remaining leaves" batch) with a `blocks` edge into close-out 0ec1, so the package can be brought to the floor and the allowlist eventually emptied.

---
FINDING 2 — Conflicting assumptions: the scoped-T201 contract this child ENABLES ("allowed in the CLI presentation layer `_cli`/`_commands`; banned in library/core/MCP") collides with ~42 pre-existing `# noqa: T201` sites that live in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` — a library/core subprocess package, NOT the CLI presentation layer.

criteria: [this child What "Enable ruff T201 (scoped) ... banned in library/core/MCP; allowed in the CLI presentation layer (_cli, _commands) via per-file-ignore"; this child AC3 "allowlist covering all un-swept packages; CI is green"; sibling fd72 (CLI T201 carve-out); sibling 0569 (reconciler sweep)]
location: ~42 `# noqa: T201` sites in src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile.py:285..1280, applier.py, batch_dispatch.py, apply_inbound.py, outbound_differ.py, acli.py, invariants.py — grep confirmed). The reconciler is under `_engine/`, NOT under `_cli`/`_commands`.
evidence:
  - This child's only named permanent T201 carve-out is the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`). The reconciler is a separate library/core subprocess package with ~42 print sites already annotated `# noqa: T201`.
  - For THIS child's "CI is green" at enable-time, those reconciler T201 sites can stay green ONLY via the un-swept-package allowlist exemption — i.e. this child's authored allowlist must scope T201 (not only BLE001) for `rebar_reconciler/`. The child's What couples the allowlist to "BLE001 and T201" but its example carve-out language ("allowed in the CLI presentation layer") risks an author treating T201 as needing only the CLI per-file-ignore + no reconciler exemption, which red-CIs the reconciler at enable-time.
scenarios:
  - Author enables T201, adds the CLI per-file-ignore, but does not put `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` in the (un-swept) T201 allowlist: the reconciler is un-swept and must be allowlisted; if the authored allowlist is BLE001-only and not T201, this child's CI-green AC fails on the reconciler.
  - Note: the deeper reconciler dispute (migrate-vs-keep prints, handler-less `rebar_reconciler.*` namespace, the `^ERROR:` grep contract) is owned by 0569 / close-out 0ec1 and is already filed there — NOT re-attributed to this pairing. The finding here is narrowly that THIS child enables the scoped rule whose only documented T201 allow is the CLI layer, while a library/core package (`rebar_reconciler/`) carries ~42 T201 sites that this child's allowlist must also scope for T201 to keep enable-time CI green.
impact: The scoped-T201 enablement this child authors is under-specified for the un-swept reconciler package: unless the authored allowlist applies to T201 (not only BLE001) for `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, turning T201 on red-CIs ~42 sites, contradicting this child's own AC3 "CI is green."
checklist_item: conflicting assumptions
suggested_fix: Make explicit in this child's What/AC that the authored per-file-ignores allowlist scopes BOTH BLE001 and T201 for every un-swept package (name `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` as a T201-and-BLE001 allowlist entry), distinct from the permanent CLI-presentation-layer T201 per-file-ignore. Add an enable-time check that `ruff` is clean across the repo immediately after enabling, with the reconciler covered by the un-swept allowlist (not by the CLI carve-out).

---
Modes checked and clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (this child authors the allowlist; sweeps mutate it later behind `blocks` edges 16a1 -> each sweep -> 0ec1, so no concurrent writer at author-time); scope overlap (exemplar remediation of `llm/plan_review/` is uniquely this child's — grep confirms all of that package's ~30 except sites and the seeded BLE001 noqas are in-package; no sibling touches it); ordering violation (the "land before the sweeps, lock the lint first" ordering is correctly encoded: 8fbd blocks 16a1; 16a1 blocks 8d01/fd72/d360/ceaa/0569; chain consistent); consumer impact (the stdout/JSON-RPC data contract is owned by 8fbd's purity test + fd72's CLI carve-out; this exemplar package has NO stdout/stderr data prints — grep shows zero real `print(` and zero `file=sys.stderr` in plan_review/, only `material_fingerprint` substring matches — so AC2 "migrate stderr-diagnostic prints" is vacuously satisfied, benign, not a contradiction); residual references (no dangling reference introduced by this pairing). The two findings above are the dependency-gap/scope-coverage and conflicting-assumptions modes.
